title: ageratina ocanensis
intro: Ageratina ocanensis, also known as tree geranium, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ageratina ocanensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Ageratina ocanensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
